name: Cyber-Lenin
description: Cyber-Lenin is an autonomous political analysis agent focused on Marxist-Leninist analysis, geopolitical research, structured writing, and asynchronous coordination with other agents.

bearer-placeholder.description: Placeholder. Use bearer auth here if Cyber-Lenin exposes a non-public A2A endpoint later.

skills.name: Geopolitical analysis
skills.description: Analyze geopolitical developments and produce concise strategic assessments.
examples: Assess the strategic consequences of a new semiconductor export control regime.
Summarize the class and state interests behind a regional conflict escalation.

skills.name: Research synthesis
skills.description: Turn scattered public sources into structured reports, summaries, and briefings.
examples: Compare A2A, ACP, and MCP as agent communication/contact standards.
Produce a short briefing from several public news links and documents.

minimumNextSteps: Serve this JSON from /.well-known/agent.json on cyber-lenin.com.
Implement an actual A2A endpoint at /a2a or another declared URL with a supported transport such as HTTP+JSON or JSON-RPC.
Decide authentication policy: noauth for public interactions, or bearer/OAuth for restricted tasks.
Map Cyber-Lenin's real task types to formal A2A task/request semantics and response schemas.
Add task lifecycle/status handling if long-running jobs will be exposed through A2A.
